principle: Next.js: use CLI build script for Turbopack, legacy plugin for Webpack
explanation: Modern: `yarn add -D @tamagui/cli`, build script `tamagui build --target web ./src -- next build`. Configures transpilation for `@tamagui/lucide-icons-2`, sets Turbopack aliases for RN compatibility. Legacy Webpack: `withTamagui` plugin from `@tamagui/next-plugin`. Use `@tamagui/next-theme` with `useRootTheme` hook for SSR-safe light/dark.

principle: Expo: use template, babel plugin, and Inter font
explanation: `yarn create tamagui@latest --template expo-router` (Yarn 4.4.0+). Manual: install `@tamagui/babel-plugin`, add to `babel.config.js`. Web: follow Metro guide. Fonts via `@tamagui/font-inter` or `@expo-google-fonts/inter` + `useFonts` hook. Clear cache on first run: `npx expo start -c`. Add `userInterfaceStyle: "automatic"` to app.config.ts for dark mode.

principle: Vite: @tamagui/vite-plugin requires ESM
explanation: Set `"type": "module"` in package.json. Add to `vite.config.ts`: `tamaguiPlugin({ config: './tamagui.config.ts', components: ['tamagui'] })`. Plugin reads `tamagui.build.ts` for shared config. React Native packages may need additional alias mappings for RNW compatibility.

principle: Metro/React Native: @tamagui/babel-plugin is fully optional
explanation: Tamagui works without any babel config on native ŌĆö just `TamaguiProvider`. The babel plugin adds compiler optimizations: CSS extraction isn't applicable, but it can flatten styled components and hoist constants. Configure in `babel.config.js` with `config`, `components`, and `exclude: /node_modules/`.

principle: Design system package: own npm module + tamagui-build + components array
explanation: Design systems need their own package (can be private) so the compiler can import and analyze them. Use `@tamagui/build` (wraps esbuild+tsc) to build with JSX preserved. Set `module:jsx` and `sideEffects: ['*.css']` in package.json. Then add the package name to `components: ['@yourapp/ui', 'tamagui']` in bundler config.

principle: Design system pattern: styled() with variants + GetProps type export
explanation: Create components with `styled(YStack, { variants: {ŌĆ”} })`, export `export type CircleProps = GetProps<typeof Circle>`. Re-export base components from `tamagui` or `@tamagui/core` for a single import surface. Test with `// debug` pragma to verify extraction: output should say `1 optimized, 1 flattened`.

principle: createFont: align keys across size, lineHeight, letterSpacing, weight
explanation: Aligning the same key names across all font sub-categories enables SizableText `size` prop to look up matching values from all categories simultaneously. Example: size: { $4: 16 }, lineHeight: { $4: 24 } ŌĆö `<SizableText size="$4" />` picks both. Use `createFont({ family, size, lineHeight, weight, letterSpacing, transform, style, color })` inside `createTamagui({ fonts: { body, heading } })`.

principle: v2 breaking change: animation ŌåÆ transition, space ŌåÆ gap, tag ŌåÆ render
explanation: Renamed props in v2: `animation` prop ŌåÆ `transition`, `space`/`spaceDirection` ŌåÆ `gap`, `tag` ŌåÆ `render`, `Stack` ŌåÆ `View`, `ellipse` ŌåÆ `numberOfLines`. Shadow props removed ŌĆö use `boxShadow`. All RN accessibility props ŌåÆ ARIA equivalents. `Group` children now need `Group.Item` wrapper. `Input` migrated to web-standard HTML attrs.

principle: v2 breaking change: media query naming and flex defaults
explanation: Config v5 media renames: `$2xl` ŌåÆ `$xxl`, `$2xs` ŌåÆ `$xxs`. Max queries: kebab-case (`max-xxl`). New height queries (`height-sm/md/lg`) and device queries (`touch`, `hoverable`). Flex behavior: `flexBasis` now defaults to `0` (RN-aligned). `position` defaults to browser `static` (not `relative`). Bundle size: ~32% smaller (37KB ŌåÆ 25KB gzipped).

principle: unstyled prop removes all Tamagui default styles
explanation: Every Tamagui UI component accepts `unstyled` ŌĆö removes all default padding, colors, border radius etc. Useful when you want the accessibility/behavior layer without the visual defaults. Pair with your own styled() wrapper to add custom styling.

principle: asChild passes all props to the single child element
explanation: `asChild` on any Tamagui component renders without a wrapper ŌĆö it passes all props (style, events, theme) to its direct child. `<Popover.Trigger asChild><MyButton /></Popover.Trigger>` makes MyButton open the Popover. Child must accept the passed props. Similar to Radix UI's asChild pattern.

principle: render prop: change the rendered element type
explanation: v2 replaces `tag` with `render`. Three forms: (1) string tag name `render="a"` ŌĆö compiler-optimized; (2) JSX element `render={<a href="/">}` ŌĆö not compiler-optimizable (deopt); (3) render function `render={(props) => <a {...props} />}` ŌĆö full control. For HTML anchor links use `<View render="a" href="/" />`.

principle: .styleable() wraps functional components for use with styled()
explanation: When building a compound component, your functional component needs `.styleable()` wrapper so it can be passed to `styled()` later: `const Button = ButtonFrame.styleable(({children, ...props}, ref) => <ButtonFrame ref={ref} {...props}><ButtonText>{children}</ButtonText></ButtonFrame>)`. This ensures prop forwarding, variant merging, and ref forwarding work correctly.

principle: accept: wrap non-Tamagui components (SVG, third-party) for token support
explanation: `styled(SvgIcon, { accept: { fill: 'color' } })` ŌĆö the `accept` option maps non-Tamagui prop names to Tamagui style categories, enabling token values like `fill="$blue10"`. Set `acceptsClassName: true` on web for CSS media query support and compile-time optimization for external components.

principle: forceStyle prop activates a pseudo state programmatically
explanation: `<View forceStyle="hover">` forces hover styles even without hovering ŌĆö useful for demos, visual regression tests, Storybook stories, or keyboard-driven navigation where you want the hover appearance without mouse presence.

principle: themeInverse flips lightŌåödark within any subtree
explanation: `<View themeInverse>` inverts the current theme for the subtree. If the root is dark, the children get light; vice versa. Useful for contrast sections or call-to-action areas that need to stand out from the rest of the page.

principle: Exports: platform detection constants and helper hooks
explanation: @tamagui/core exports: `isWeb`, `isBrowser`, `isServer`, `isClient`, `isChrome`, `isWebTouchable`, `isTouchable`, `useIsomorphicLayoutEffect`. Helpers: `getConfig`, `getTokens`, `getToken`, `getTokenValue`, `getVariable` (CSS var on web, raw on native), `isTamaguiComponent`, `isTamaguiElement`. Type helpers: `GetProps`, `GetRef`.

principle: useProps and useStyle hooks extract computed values from styled components
explanation: `useProps(props)` returns the fully-resolved props after merging variants and theme. `useStyle(props)` returns just the style object. `usePropsAndStyle(props)` returns both. Useful when you need to pass resolved styles to third-party components or for debugging what a component actually produces.

principle: Radix Colors v3 in v5: 10+ palettes, improved accessibility
explanation: V5 config adds orange, pink, purple, teal, gray, neutral color themes built on Radix Colors v3. Includes shadow scales, highlight variants, opacity adjustments. Use `createV5Theme({ palettes: { accent: myPalette } })` to swap any palette. `adjustPalette` utilities transform lightness/saturation programmatically.

principle: useThemeName returns the current resolved theme name string
explanation: `const name = useThemeName()` returns the current theme name (e.g. `'dark_green'`). Useful for conditional logic based on theme without subscribing to all theme values. Lighter alternative to `useTheme()` when you only need the name.
